rationale: The citing paper does more than list this reference; it explicitly states that MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) found emotional messages to be more effective in mature categories. That finding is used in the argument that brand-level (more persuasive/emotional) messages should become relatively more effective later in the life cycle, shaping the paper's theoretical expectations. Although the cited study is not reused methodologically, it meaningfully informs the citing paper's conceptual framing of message-content dynamics.
relation_description: The citing paper uses MacInnis, Rao, and Weiss (2002) as prior empirical support for the idea that emotional versus informational message content changes effectiveness over market maturity. This supports the citing paper's setup and predictions about when brand-level versus category-level advertising should work better.
